pattern: Treating the array as a ring just means one extra neighbor pair wraps from the last element back to the first, so scanning every consecutive pair including that wraparound one and tracking the largest absolute gap solves it directly.
hints: Every element has exactly two neighbors in a circular array except that the wraparound pair (last, first) is easy to forget.
Walk the array once comparing each element to the one right after it, and don't stop before also comparing the last element back to the first.
Keep a running maximum of the absolute difference across all of those adjacent pairs, including the wraparound one -- that running maximum is the answer.

edge_inputs.description: statement example, wraparound pair gives the max
two equal elements, difference is zero both ways
the largest gap is between consecutive interior elements, not the wraparound pair
extremes at both ends of the array

stress.description: random arrays across the full value range and a range of lengths
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    n = max(2, min(size, 100))
    nums = [rng.randint(-100, 100) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
